mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate and tightens corporate borrowing conditions, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and consumer credit expansion indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in 100+ walk-forward windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates exceed 4%, elevated borrowing costs amplify the negative impact of slowing housing starts on corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and HOUST declines, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not statistically higher than contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in non-farm payrolls signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering earnings downgrades that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The market rallies or remains flat following sharp payroll declines, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with stable or falling consumer prices indicates improving supply chain efficiency and disinflationary growth, which expands profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL stabilizes, or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% while unemployment remains elevated, the combination signals stagflationary risks that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive despite low GDP growth and high unemployment, or the signal fails to distinguish stagflationary periods from healthy slowdowns in forward testing.
